*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: lib/preFlight.ts
+import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
+import { and, desc, eq, gte, lte } from "drizzle-orm";
+import { db } from "../db";
+import {
+  agencies,
+  agents,
+  artists,
+  dealClarifications,
+  deals,
+  settlements,
+  shows,
+  venues,
+  type Recoup,
+} from "../db/schema";
+
+const MODEL = "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18";
+
+export type MarketingRecoupCapRisk = {
+  riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
+  severity: "high" | "medium";
+  leadSentence: string;
+  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+  suggestedClarification: string;
+  citationShowIds: string[];
+};
+
+export type PreFlightDetectionContext = Awaited<
+  ReturnType<typeof getShowContextForDetection>
+>;
+
+type RawModelRisk = {
+  riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
+  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+  suggestedClarification: string;
+  citationShowIds: string[];
+};
+
+type RawModelResponse = {
+  risks: RawModelRisk[];
+};
+
+function todayLocalDateString() {
+  const d = new Date();
+  const year = d.getFullYear();
+  const month = String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0");
+  const day = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, "0");
+  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
+}
+
+function addMonths(dateString: string, months: number) {
+  const [year, month, day] = dateString.split("-").map(Number);
+  const d = new Date(year, month - 1, day);
+  d.setMonth(d.getMonth() + months);
+  const outYear = d.getFullYear();
+  const outMonth = String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0");
+  const outDay = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, "0");
+  return `${outYear}-${outMonth}-${outDay}`;
+}
+
+function amountFromMarketingRecoupPhrase(phrase: string) {
+  const amountMatch = phrase.match(/\$([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/);
+  if (!amountMatch) return null;
+  return Number(amountMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ""));
+}
+
+function formatMoneyNumber(value: number) {
+  return value.toLocaleString("en-US", {
+    maximumFractionDigits: 0,
+    minimumFractionDigits: 0,
+  });
+}
+
+function normalizedPhrase(value: string) {
+  return value.trim().replace(/\s+/g, " ").toLowerCase();
+}
+
+function containsLiteralSubstring(prose: string, phrase: string) {
+  return prose.toLowerCase().includes(phrase.toLowerCase());
+}
+
+export async function getShowContextForDetection(showId: string) {
+  const rows = await db
+    .select({
+      show: shows,
+      deal: deals,
+      artist: artists,
+      agent: agents,
+      agency: agencies,
+      settlement: settlements,
+      venue: venues,
+    })
+    .from(shows)
+    .leftJoin(deals, eq(deals.showId, shows.id))
+    .leftJoin(artists, eq(shows.artistId, artists.id))
+    .leftJoin(agents, eq(artists.agentId, agents.id))
+    .leftJoin(agencies, eq(agents.agencyId, agencies.id))
+    .leftJoin(settlements, eq(settlements.showId, shows.id))
+    .leftJoin(venues, eq(shows.venueId, venues.id))
+    .where(eq(shows.id, showId));
+
+  if (rows.length === 0) {
+    throw new Error(`Show not found: ${showId}`);
+  }
+  const row = rows[0];
+  if (!row.deal) {
+    throw new Error(`Show has no deal: ${showId}`);
+  }
+  if (!row.venue) {
+    throw new Error(`Show has no venue: ${showId}`);
+  }
+
+  const asOfDate = row.show.date < todayLocalDateString()
+    ? row.show.date
+    : todayLocalDateString();
+  const windowStart = addMonths(asOfDate, -18);
+
+  const recoupRows = await db
+    .select({
+      show: shows,
+      artist: artists,
+      agent: agents,
+      agency: agencies,
+      settlement: settlements,
+    })
+    .from(settlements)
+    .innerJoin(shows, eq(settlements.showId, shows.id))
+    .leftJoin(artists, eq(shows.artistId, artists.id))
+    .leftJoin(agents, eq(artists.agentId, agents.id))
+    .leftJoin(agencies, eq(agents.agencyId, agencies.id))
+    .where(
+      and(
+        eq(shows.venueId, row.venue.id),
+        gte(shows.date, windowStart),
+        lte(shows.date, asOfDate),
+      ),
+    )
+    .orderBy(desc(shows.date));
+
+  const pastDisputes = recoupRows
+    .flatMap((r) => {
+      if (!r.settlement.recoupsJson) return [];
+      let recoups: Recoup[];
+      try {
+        const parsed = JSON.parse(r.settlement.recoupsJson);
+        if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return [];
+        recoups = parsed;
+      } catch {
+        return [];
+      }
+
+      return recoups
+        .filter(
+          (recoup) =>
+            recoup.category === "marketing" && recoup.status === "disputed",
+        )
+        .map((recoup) => ({
+          showId: r.show.id,
+          date: r.show.date,
+          artistName: r.artist?.name ?? null,
+          agentName: r.agent?.name ?? null,
+          agencyName: r.agency?.name ?? null,
+          recoupLabel: recoup.label,
+          disputedAmount: recoup.amount,
+          settlementStatus: r.settlement.status,
+        }));
+    })
+    .slice(0, 8);
+
+  return {
+    target: {
+      showId: row.show.id,
+      date: row.show.date,
+      venueName: row.venue.name,
+      artistName: row.artist?.name ?? null,
+      agentName: row.agent?.name ?? null,
+      agencyName: row.agency?.name ?? null,
+      agentProfileNote: row.agent?.preferencesNotes ?? null,
+      deal: {
+        id: row.deal.id,
+        dealType: row.deal.dealType,
+        guaranteeAmount: row.deal.guaranteeAmount,
+        percentage: row.deal.percentage,
+        percentageBasis: row.deal.percentageBasis,
+        expenseCap: row.deal.expenseCap,
+        hospitalityCap: row.deal.hospitalityCap,
+        bonusesJson: row.deal.bonusesJson,
+        dealNotesFreetext: row.deal.dealNotesFreetext ?? "",
+      },
+      settlementStatus: row.settlement?.status ?? null,
+    },
+    pastDisputes,
+  };
+}
+
+export async function detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
+  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
+): Promise<MarketingRecoupCapRisk[]> {
+  const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
+  if (!prose || context.target.deal.expenseCap == null) return [];
+
+  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
+  if (!apiKey) {
+    throw new Error("OPENAI_API_KEY is required to run pre-flight detection.");
+  }
+
+  const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
+    method: "POST",
+    headers: {
+      "content-type": "application/json",
+      authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify({
+      model: MODEL,
+      temperature: 0.1,
+      response_format: {
+        type: "json_schema",
+        json_schema: {
+          name: "marketing_recoup_cap_detection",
+          strict: true,
+          schema: {
+            type: "object",
+            additionalProperties: false,
+            required: ["risks"],
+            properties: {
+              risks: {
+                type: "array",
+                items: {
+                  type: "object",
+                  additionalProperties: false,
+                  required: [
+                    "riskClass",
+                    "detectedPhraseFromDeal",
+                    "suggestedClarification",
+                    "citationShowIds",
+                  ],
+                  properties: {
+                    riskClass: {
+                      type: "string",
+                      enum: ["marketing_recoup_cap"],
+                    },
+                    detectedPhraseFromDeal: { type: "string" },
+                    suggestedClarification: { type: "string" },
+                    citationShowIds: {
+                      type: "array",
+                      items: { type: "string" },
+                    },
+                  },
+                },
+              },
+            },
+          },
+        },
+      },
+      messages: [
+        {
+          role: "system",
+          content: [
+            "You detect only the marketing_recoup_cap risk class for Greenroom pre-flight deal review.",
+            "Hard rules:",
+            "1. Never invent values, dates, agency names, amounts, or show IDs.",
+            "2. detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.",
+            "3. citationShowIds must come only from the provided pastDisputes input.",
+            "4. Return strict JSON matching the schema.",
+            "5. No prose explanations outside JSON.",
+          ].join("\n"),
+        },
+        {
+          role: "user",
+          content: JSON.stringify({
+            task: "Return one risk only if the target deal prose includes a marketing recoup and the structured deal has an expense cap. Otherwise return an empty risks array.",
+            target: context.target,
+            pastDisputes: context.pastDisputes,
+          }),
+        },
+      ],
+    }),
+  });
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    const text = await response.text();
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI request failed (${response.status}): ${text}`);
+  }
+
+  const completion = await response.json();
+  const message = completion.choices?.[0]?.message;
+  if (message?.refusal) {
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI refusal: ${message.refusal}`);
+  }
+  if (!message?.content) return [];
+
+  const parsed = JSON.parse(message.content) as RawModelResponse;
+  const citationIds = new Set(context.pastDisputes.map((d) => d.showId));
+  const completePastDisputes = context.pastDisputes.filter(
+    (d) => d.agencyName && d.date && typeof d.disputedAmount === "number",
+  );
+
+  return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): MarketingRecoupCapRisk[] => {
+    if (risk.riskClass !== "marketing_recoup_cap") return [];
+    if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+
+    const allowedCitations = risk.citationShowIds.filter((id) =>
+      citationIds.has(id),
+    );
+    const recoupAmount = amountFromMarketingRecoupPhrase(
+      risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+    );
+    const expenseCap = context.target.deal.expenseCap;
+
+    if (completePastDisputes.length > 0 && recoupAmount != null) {
+      const cited =
+        completePastDisputes.find((d) => allowedCitations.includes(d.showId)) ??
+        completePastDisputes[0];
+
+      return [
+        {
+          riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap",
+          severity: "high",
+          leadSentence:
+            `$${formatMoneyNumber(recoupAmount)} marketing recoup × ` +
+            `$${formatMoneyNumber(expenseCap)} expense cap. Same combination ` +
+            `contested at this venue with ${cited.agencyName} on ${cited.date} ` +
+            `over a $${formatMoneyNumber(cited.disputedAmount)} recoup.`,
+          detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+          suggestedClarification: risk.suggestedClarification,
+          citationShowIds: allowedCitations.length
+            ? allowedCitations
+            : [cited.showId],
+        },
+      ];
+    }
+
+    return [
+      {
+        riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap",
+        severity: "medium",
+        leadSentence:
+          `Marketing recoup × expense cap. Contested ${context.pastDisputes.length} ` +
+          "times at this venue in the last 18 months.",
+        detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+        suggestedClarification: risk.suggestedClarification,
+        citationShowIds: allowedCitations,
+      },
+    ];
+  });
+}
+
+export async function persistMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
+  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
+  risks: MarketingRecoupCapRisk[],
+) {
+  const existing = await db
+    .select()
+    .from(dealClarifications)
+    .where(
+      and(
+        eq(dealClarifications.dealId, context.target.deal.id),
+        eq(dealClarifications.riskClass, "marketing_recoup_cap"),
+      ),
+    );
+
+  const existingPhrases = new Set(
+    existing.map((row) => normalizedPhrase(row.detectedPhraseFromDeal)),
+  );
+  const inserted: string[] = [];
+
+  for (const risk of risks) {
+    if (existingPhrases.has(normalizedPhrase(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal))) {
+      continue;
+    }
+
+    const hash = createHash("sha1")
+      .update(
+        `${context.target.deal.id}|${risk.riskClass}|${normalizedPhrase(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)}`,
+      )
+      .digest("hex")
+      .slice(0, 10);
+    const id = `clr_${context.target.deal.id}_${risk.riskClass}_${hash}`;
+
+    await db.insert(dealClarifications).values({
+      id,
+      dealId: context.target.deal.id,
+      riskClass: risk.riskClass,
+      severity: risk.severity,
+      leadSentence: risk.leadSentence,
+      detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+      suggestedClarification: risk.suggestedClarification,
+      citationShowIds: JSON.stringify(risk.citationShowIds),
+      detectedAt: new Date(),
+      detectedByModel: MODEL,
+      status: "pending",
+    });
+    inserted.push(id);
+    existingPhrases.add(normalizedPhrase(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal));
+  }
+
+  return inserted;
+}
*** End Patch
````

*** Add File: scripts/verify-preflight.ts
+import { readFileSync, existsSync } from "node:fs";
+import {
+  detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks,
+  getShowContextForDetection,
+  persistMarketingRecoupCapRisks,
+} from "../lib/preFlight";
+import { client } from "../db";
+
+function loadEnvLocal() {
+  const path = ".env.local";
+  if (!existsSync(path)) return;
+
+  const raw = readFileSync(path, "utf8");
+  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
+    const trimmed = line.trim();
+    if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith("#")) continue;
+    const match = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)=(.*)$/);
+    if (!match) continue;
+
+    const [, key, rawValue] = match;
+    if (process.env[key] != null) continue;
+
+    let value = rawValue.trim();
+    if (
+      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
+      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))
+    ) {
+      value = value.slice(1, -1);
+    }
+    process.env[key] = value;
+  }
+}
+
+function truncate(value: string, max = 700) {
+  if (value.length <= max) return value;
+  return `${value.slice(0, max)}... [truncated ${value.length - max} chars]`;
+}
+
+function contextForPrint(
+  context: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getShowContextForDetection>>,
+) {
+  return {
+    ...context,
+    target: {
+      ...context.target,
+      deal: {
+        ...context.target.deal,
+        dealNotesFreetext: truncate(context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext),
+        bonusesJson: context.target.deal.bonusesJson
+          ? truncate(context.target.deal.bonusesJson, 300)
+          : null,
+      },
+    },
+  };
+}
+
+async function main() {
+  loadEnvLocal();
+
+  const args = process.argv.slice(2);
+  const persist = args.includes("--persist");
+  const showId = args.find((arg) => !arg.startsWith("--"));
+  if (!showId) {
+    throw new Error("Usage: npm run preflight:check -- <showId> [--persist]");
+  }
+
+  const context = await getShowContextForDetection(showId);
+  console.log("=== LLM input context ===");
+  console.log(JSON.stringify(contextForPrint(context), null, 2));
+
+  const risks = await detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks(context);
+  console.log("\n=== Detected risks ===");
+  console.log(JSON.stringify(risks, null, 2));
+
+  if (persist) {
+    const inserted = await persistMarketingRecoupCapRisks(context, risks);
+    console.log("\n=== Persisted clarification IDs ===");
+    console.log(JSON.stringify(inserted, null, 2));
+  }
+}
+
+main()
+  .then(() => {
+    client.close();
+    process.exit(0);
+  })
+  .catch((error) => {
+    console.error(error);
+    client.close();
+    process.exit(1);
+  });
*** End Patch

<a id="turn-09"></a>

## Turn 09 — Phase 3 — Marketing detector and false-positive fix

- Timestamp: `2026-05-21T12:59:23.568Z`
- Turn ID: `019e4a9e-8621-7981-93bf-27fb3fc2b98c`

### User prompt

````
Awesome stuff. The detection works.  Lead sentence nails the template. I don't like the consultancy-speak also. No booker would send that.

Fix matches the architectural pattern. Take it off the LLM and template it deterministically. Same reasoning as why bonus_structure_drift and agent_risk_pattern are deterministic: the question is mechanical, not language-judgment.
Edit detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks so suggestedClarification is built in code. Template:

Quick clarification before [SHOW_DATE]: is the $[recoup] marketing recoup inside or outside the $[cap] expense cap?

SHOW_DATE formatted naturally ("Mar 14" or similar). Pull the date from the show context.

Remove suggested_clarification from the LLM's JSON schema entirely so the model isn't asked to produce it. Smaller payload, more deterministic, no garbage output.

Re-run on Coastal Spell and show me the output.
````

### Agent messages

#### Agent message 1

````
I’ll move `suggestedClarification` fully out of the model contract and build it from show date, recoup amount, and cap in code. Then I’ll rerun the Coastal Spell check so we can inspect the new output.
````

#### Agent message 2

````
The LLM schema no longer includes `suggestedClarification`, and the question is now deterministic. I’m running TypeScript and the Coastal check.
````

#### Agent message 3

````
The fallback branch can only template the question if the model phrase includes a recoup amount. I’m tightening the guard so risks without a parseable recoup amount are rejected instead of trying to build an under-specified question.
````

#### Agent message 4

````
TypeScript passes. I’m rerunning the Coastal Spell CLI now to verify the output uses the deterministic booker-ready question.
````

#### Agent message 5

````
Updated `detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks` so `suggestedClarification` is no longer generated by the LLM. It’s now deterministic from `showDate`, parsed recoup amount, and `expenseCap`.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

Coastal Spell output:

```json
[
  {
    "riskClass": "marketing_recoup_cap",
    "severity": "high",
    "leadSentence": "$900 marketing recoup × $2,500 expense cap. Same combination contested at this venue with Wasserman on 2025-03-14 over a $900 recoup.",
    "detectedPhraseFromDeal": "Marketing recoup of $900 against gross.",
    "suggestedClarification": "Quick clarification before Mar 14: is the $900 marketing recoup inside or outside the $2,500 expense cap?",
    "citationShowIds": [
      "show_coastal_spell_dispute"
    ]
  }
]
```
````

@@
 type RawModelRisk = {
   riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
   detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
-  suggestedClarification: string;
   citationShowIds: string[];
 };
@@
 function formatMoneyNumber(value: number) {
   return value.toLocaleString("en-US", {
     maximumFractionDigits: 0,
     minimumFractionDigits: 0,
   });
 }
+
+function formatShortShowDate(dateString: string) {
+  const [year, month, day] = dateString.split("-").map(Number);
+  return new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", {
+    month: "short",
+    day: "numeric",
+  }).format(new Date(year, month - 1, day));
+}
+
+function marketingRecoupCapQuestion(input: {
+  showDate: string;
+  recoupAmount: number;
+  expenseCap: number;
+}) {
+  return (
+    `Quick clarification before ${formatShortShowDate(input.showDate)}: ` +
+    `is the $${formatMoneyNumber(input.recoupAmount)} marketing recoup ` +
+    `inside or outside the $${formatMoneyNumber(input.expenseCap)} expense cap?`
+  );
+}
@@
                   required: [
                     "riskClass",
                     "detectedPhraseFromDeal",
-                    "suggestedClarification",
                     "citationShowIds",
                   ],
                   properties: {
@@

-                    suggestedClarification: { type: "string" },
                     citationShowIds: {
                       type: "array",
                       items: { type: "string" },
@@
             `$${formatMoneyNumber(expenseCap)} expense cap. Same combination ` +
             `contested at this venue with ${cited.agencyName} on ${cited.date} ` +
             `over a $${formatMoneyNumber(cited.disputedAmount)} recoup.`,
           detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
-          suggestedClarification: risk.suggestedClarification,
+          suggestedClarification: marketingRecoupCapQuestion({
+            showDate: context.target.date,
+            recoupAmount,

             ? allowedCitations
@@
         leadSentence:
           `Marketing recoup × expense cap. Contested ${context.pastDisputes.length} ` +

         detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
-        suggestedClarification: risk.suggestedClarification,

<a id="turn-11"></a>

## Turn 11 — Phase 3 — Marketing detector and false-positive fix

- Timestamp: `2026-05-21T13:31:09.868Z`
- Turn ID: `019e4abb-9c78-7971-8d83-1fbb67b28e6c`

### User prompt

````
Three false positives across the regression suite. The detector is firing on the presence of an expense_cap field, not on actual recoup language in the prose.

Breakdown:
- show_coastal_spell_dispute: correct HIGH. It detected the $900 marketing recoup and used the locked template.
- show_0141: correct HIGH. It parsed the $550 marketing recoup from prose and cited real WME precedent.
- show_0064: false positive. No recoup or marketing language in prose. Detector fired MEDIUM on the mere presence of an expense_cap.
- show_0001: false positive. Door deal, no recoup or marketing language in prose. Detector fabricated a $750 marketing recoup from the expense cap.
- show_0010: false positive. No recoup or marketing language in prose. Detector fired HIGH because the show had a recoup dispute in its own history.

The bug:
The detector is firing on structured expense_cap presence, not on actual recoup language in dealNotesFreetext. The LLM is too eager. The literal substring guard caught some fabricated phrases, but not phrases like "Expenses capped $2350" because that phrase really is in the prose. It just is not a marketing recoup phrase.

Make a two-layer deterministic fix:

1. Pre-LLM gate:
   Skip the LLM call entirely if dealNotesFreetext does not contain "recoup" or "marketing" case-insensitively. This saves the call and prevents the model from over-firing.

2. Post-LLM gate:
   Reject the risk if detectedPhraseFromDeal does not contain "recoup" or "marketing" case-insensitively. This is defense in depth.

Also exclude the target show from its own pastDisputes when building context. A show should never cite itself as precedent. Both show_coastal_spell_dispute and show_0010 did this in the output above.

Make only those three changes. Do not generalize to other detectors yet.

Then re-run the same five-show regression:
- show_0064 should return zero risks.
- show_0001 should return zero risks.
- show_0010 should return zero risks.
- show_coastal_spell_dispute should still return HIGH, but with cleaner citations that do not include itself.
- show_0141 should still return HIGH with the same lead sentence or an equivalent locked-template sentence.

Show me the diff before applying.
````

### Agent messages

#### Agent message 1

````
I’ll prepare this as a proposed diff only: one pre-call phrase gate, one post-call phrase gate, and one context filter that removes the target show from precedent rows. No edits applied yet.
````

#### Agent message 2

````
Proposed diff only, not applied:

```diff

--- a/lib/preFlight.ts
+++ b/lib/preFlight.ts
@@
 function containsLiteralSubstring(prose: string, phrase: string) {
   return prose.toLowerCase().includes(phrase.toLowerCase());
 }
+
+function containsMarketingRecoupSignal(value: string) {
+  const normalized = value.toLowerCase();
+  return normalized.includes("recoup") || normalized.includes("marketing");
+}
 
 export async function getShowContextForDetection(showId: string) {
@@
   const pastDisputes = recoupRows
+    .filter((r) => r.show.id !== showId)
     .flatMap((r) => {
       if (!r.settlement.recoupsJson) return [];
       let recoups: Recoup[];
@@
 export async function detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
   context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
 ): Promise<MarketingRecoupCapRisk[]> {
   const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
   if (!prose || context.target.deal.expenseCap == null) return [];
+  if (!containsMarketingRecoupSignal(prose)) return [];
 
   const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
   if (!apiKey) {
@@
   return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): MarketingRecoupCapRisk[] => {
     if (risk.riskClass !== "marketing_recoup_cap") return [];
     if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+    if (!containsMarketingRecoupSignal(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
 
     const allowedCitations = risk.citationShowIds.filter((id) =>
       citationIds.has(id),
     );

Output:
import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
import { and, desc, eq, gte, lte } from "drizzle-orm";
import { db } from "../db";
import {
  agencies,
  agents,
  artists,
  dealClarifications,
  deals,
  settlements,
  shows,
  venues,
  type Recoup,
} from "../db/schema";

const MODEL = "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18";

export type MarketingRecoupCapRisk = {
  riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
  severity: "high" | "medium";
  leadSentence: string;
  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
  suggestedClarification: string;
  citationShowIds: string[];
};

export type PreFlightDetectionContext = Awaited<
  ReturnType<typeof getShowContextForDetection>
>;

type RawModelRisk = {
  riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
  citationShowIds: string[];
};

type RawModelResponse = {
  risks: RawModelRisk[];
};

function todayLocalDateString() {
  const d = new Date();
  const year = d.getFullYear();
  const month = String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0");
  const day = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, "0");
  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
}

function addMonths(dateString: string, months: number) {
  const [year, month, day] = dateString.split("-").map(Number);
  const d = new Date(year, month - 1, day);
  d.setMonth(d.getMonth() + months);
  const outYear = d.getFullYear();
  const outMonth = String(d.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0");
  const outDay = String(d.getDate()).padStart(2, "0");
  return `${outYear}-${outMonth}-${outDay}`;
}

function amountFromMarketingRecoupPhrase(phrase: string) {
  const amountMatch = phrase.match(/\$([\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/);
  if (!amountMatch) return null;
  return Number(amountMatch[1].replace(/,/g, ""));
}

function formatMoneyNumber(value: number) {
  return value.toLocaleString("en-US", {
    maximumFractionDigits: 0,
    minimumFractionDigits: 0,
  });
}

function formatShortShowDate(dateString: string) {
  const [year, month, day] = dateString.split("-").map(Number);
  return new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", {
    month: "short",
    day: "numeric",
  }).format(new Date(year, month - 1, day));
}

function marketingRecoupCapQuestion(input: {
  showDate: string;
  recoupAmount: number;
  expenseCap: number;
}) {
  return (
    `Quick clarification before ${formatShortShowDate(input.showDate)}: ` +
    `is the $${formatMoneyNumber(input.recoupAmount)} marketing recoup ` +
    `inside or outside the $${formatMoneyNumber(input.expenseCap)} expense cap?`
  );
}

function normalizedPhrase(value: string) {
  return value.trim().replace(/\s+/g, " ").toLowerCase();
}

function containsLiteralSubstring(prose: string, phrase: string) {
  return prose.toLowerCase().includes(phrase.toLowerCase());
}

export async function getShowContextForDetection(showId: string) {
  const rows = await db
    .select({
      show: shows,
      deal: deals,
      artist: artists,
      agent: agents,
      agency: agencies,
      settlement: settlements,
      venue: venues,
    })
    .from(shows)
    .leftJoin(deals, eq(deals.showId, shows.id))
    .leftJoin(artists, eq(shows.artistId, artists.id))
    .leftJoin(agents, eq(artists.agentId, agents.id))
    .leftJoin(agencies, eq(agents.agencyId, agencies.id))
    .leftJoin(settlements, eq(settlements.showId, shows.id))
    .leftJoin(venues, eq(shows.venueId, venues.id))
    .where(eq(shows.id, showId));

  if (rows.length === 0) {
    throw new Error(`Show not found: ${showId}`);
  }
  const row = rows[0];
  if (!row.deal) {
    throw new Error(`Show has no deal: ${showId}`);
  }
  if (!row.venue) {
    throw new Error(`Show has no venue: ${showId}`);
  }

  const asOfDate = row.show.date < todayLocalDateString()
    ? row.show.date
    : todayLocalDateString();
  const windowStart = addMonths(asOfDate, -18);

  const recoupRows = await db
    .select({
      show: shows,
      artist: artists,
      agent: agents,
      agency: agencies,
      settlement: settlements,
    })
    .from(settlements)
    .innerJoin(shows, eq(settlements.showId, shows.id))
    .leftJoin(artists, eq(shows.artistId, artists.id))
    .leftJoin(agents, eq(artists.agentId, agents.id))
    .leftJoin(agencies, eq(agents.agencyId, agencies.id))
    .where(
      and(
        eq(shows.venueId, row.venue.id),
        gte(shows.date, windowStart),
        lte(shows.date, asOfDate),
      ),
    )
    .orderBy(desc(shows.date));

  const pastDisputes = recoupRows
    .flatMap((r) => {
      if (!r.settlement.recoupsJson) return [];
      let recoups: Recoup[];
      try {
        const parsed = JSON.parse(r.settlement.recoupsJson);
        if (!Array.isArray(parsed)) return [];
        recoups = parsed;
      } catch {
        return [];
      }

      return recoups
        .filter(
          (recoup) =>
            recoup.category === "marketing" && recoup.status === "disputed",
        )
        .map((recoup) => ({
          showId: r.show.id,
          date: r.show.date,
          artistName: r.artist?.name ?? null,
          agentName: r.agent?.name ?? null,
          agencyName: r.agency?.name ?? null,
          recoupLabel: recoup.label,
          disputedAmount: recoup.amount,
          settlementStatus: r.settlement.status,
        }));
    })
    .slice(0, 8);

  return {
    target: {
      showId: row.show.id,
      date: row.show.date,
      venueName: row.venue.name,
      artistName: row.artist?.name ?? null,
      agentName: row.agent?.name ?? null,
      agencyName: row.agency?.name ?? null,
      agentProfileNote: row.agent?.preferencesNotes ?? null,
      deal: {
        id: row.deal.id,
        dealType: row.deal.dealType,
        guaranteeAmount: row.deal.guaranteeAmount,
        percentage: row.deal.percentage,
        percentageBasis: row.deal.percentageBasis,
        expenseCap: row.deal.expenseCap,
        hospitalityCap: row.deal.hospitalityCap,
        bonusesJson: row.deal.bonusesJson,
        dealNotesFreetext: row.deal.dealNotesFreetext ?? "",
      },
      settlementStatus: row.settlement?.status ?? null,
    },
    pastDisputes,
  };
}

export async function detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
): Promise<MarketingRecoupCapRisk[]> {
  const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
  if (!prose || context.target.deal.expenseCap == null) return [];

  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
  if (!apiKey) {
    throw new Error("OPENAI_API_KEY is required to run pre-flight detection.");
  }

  const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json",
      authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      model: MODEL,
      temperature: 0.1,
      response_format: {
        type: "json_schema",
        json_schema: {
          name: "marketing_recoup_cap_detection",
          strict: true,
          schema: {
            type: "object",
            additionalProperties: false,
            required: ["risks"],
            properties: {
              risks: {
                type: "array",
                items: {
                  type: "object",
                  additionalProperties: false,
                  required: [
                    "riskClass",
                    "detectedPhraseFromDeal",
                    "citationShowIds",
                  ],
                  properties: {
                    riskClass: {
                      type: "string",
                      enum: ["marketing_recoup_cap"],
                    },
                    detectedPhraseFromDeal: { type: "string" },
                    citationShowIds: {
                      type: "array",
                      items: { type: "string" },
                    },
                  },
                },
              },
            },
          },
        },
      },
      messages: [
        {
          role: "system",
          content: [
            "You detect only the marketing_recoup_cap risk class for Greenroom pre-flight deal review.",
            "Hard rules:",
            "1. Never invent values, dates, agency names, amounts, or show IDs.",
            "2. detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.",
            "3. citationShowIds must come only from the provided pastDisputes input.",
            "4. Return strict JSON matching the schema.",
            "5. No prose explanations outside JSON.",
          ].join("\n"),
        },
        {
          role: "user",
          content: JSON.stringify({
            task: "Return one risk only if the target deal prose includes a marketing recoup and the structured deal has an expense cap. Otherwise return an empty risks array.",
            target: context.target,
            pastDisputes: context.pastDisputes,
          }),
        },
      ],
    }),
  });

  if (!response.ok) {
    const text = await response.text();
    throw new Error(`OpenAI request failed (${response.status}): ${text}`);
  }

  const completion = await response.json();
  const message = completion.choices?.[0]?.message;
  if (message?.refusal) {
    throw new Error(`OpenAI refusal: ${message.refusal}`);
  }
  if (!message?.content) return [];

  const parsed = JSON.parse(message.content) as RawModelResponse;
  const citationIds = new Set(context.pastDisputes.map((d) => d.showId));
  const completePastDisputes = context.pastDisputes.filter(
    (d) => d.agencyName && d.date && typeof d.disputedAmount === "number",
  );

  return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): MarketingRecoupCapRisk[] => {
    if (risk.riskClass !== "marketing_recoup_cap") return [];
    if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];

    const allowedCitations = risk.citationShowIds.filter((id) =>
      citationIds.has(id),
    );
    const recoupAmount = amountFromMarketingRecoupPhrase(
      risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
    );
    const expenseCap = context.target.deal.expenseCap;
    if (expenseCap == null) return [];
    if (recoupAmount == null) return [];

    if (completePastDisputes.length > 0) {
      const cited =
        completePastDisputes.find((d) => allowedCitations.includes(d.showId)) ??
        completePastDisputes[0];

      return [
        {
          riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap",
          severity: "high",
          leadSentence:
            `$${formatMoneyNumber(recoupAmount)} marketing recoup × ` +
            `$${formatMoneyNumber(expenseCap)} expense cap. Same combination ` +
            `contested at this venue with ${cited.agencyName} on ${cited.date} ` +
            `over a $${formatMoneyNumber(cited.disputedAmount)} recoup.`,
          detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
          suggestedClarification: marketingRecoupCapQuestion({
            showDate: context.target.date,
            recoupAmount,
            expenseCap,
          }),
          citationShowIds: allowedCitations.length
            ? allowedCitations
            : [cited.showId],
        },
      ];
    }

    return [
      {
        riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap",
        severity: "medium",
        leadSentence:
          `Marketing recoup × expense cap. Contested ${context.pastDisputes.length} ` +
          "times at this venue in the last 18 months.",
        detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
        suggestedClarification: marketingRecoupCapQuestion({
          showDate: context.target.date,
          recoupAmount,
          expenseCap,
        }),
        citationShowIds: allowedCitations,
      },
    ];
  });
}

export async function persistMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
  risks: MarketingRecoupCapRisk[],
) {
  const existing = await db
    .select()
    .from(dealClarifications)

<a id="turn-13"></a>

## Turn 13 — Phase 3 — Marketing detector and false-positive fix

- Timestamp: `2026-05-21T13:40:39.663Z`
- Turn ID: `019e4ac4-4e5d-7902-88ac-f09fa69d14d5`

### User prompt

````
Regression looksclean. Keep the marketing_recoup_cap detector as the proven pattern.

Now move to the second detector: bonus_structure_drift.

Before writing code, inspect the data for bonus-related ambiguity. I want to understand the shape before we build.

Please run a focused data exploration and show results for:

1. Deals where bonusesJson is not null.
2. Deals where dealNotesFreetext mentions bonus, walkout, threshold, gross above, over $, or pot.
3. Settlements with disputed recoups or revised status where the deal also had bonus-related prose.
4. A few concrete candidate shows that would make good positive regression cases for bonus_structure_drift.
5. A few concrete negative regression cases where the deal has expense caps or hospitality caps but no bonus language.

Do not implement the detector yet. Just show the SQL/scripts and the outputs.

I am looking for the equivalent of what we just did for marketing_recoup_cap: one narrow ambiguity class, evidence from the data, and a small regression suite before implementation.
````

### Agent messages

#### Agent message 1

````
I’ll do this as read-only SQLite exploration. I’m going to run compact count queries first, then pull candidate rows with the exact prose so we can choose a regression suite before any detector work.
````

#### Agent message 2

````
The count shape is clear: structured bonuses exist mostly on `vs`, and bonus prose is also mostly `vs`. I’m pulling concrete rows now, separating structured-bonus examples, drift candidates, dispute-overlap rows, and clean negatives.
````

#### Agent message 3

````
The overlap with disputed recoups is thin and mixed, which is useful: bonus drift is lower volume than recoup ambiguity, and positive cases are better defined by prose-vs-JSON mismatch than by settlement status. I’m pulling targeted positive and negative regression candidates now.
````

#### Agent message 4

````
No detector work yet. No files changed. I ran read-only `sqlite3 -header -column data/greenroom.db` queries.

**Bonus Prose Predicate**
```sql
lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%bonus%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%walkout%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%threshold%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%gross above%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%over $%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%pot%'
```

**1. Structured Bonuses**
```sql
SELECT d.deal_type, COUNT(*) AS deals_with_structured_bonuses
FROM deals d
WHERE d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY d.deal_type
ORDER BY deals_with_structured_bonuses DESC;
```

```text
deal_type            deals_with_structured_bonuses
vs                   103
flat                 35
percentage_of_gross  6
percentage_of_net    5
```

Total structured bonus deals: `149`.

**2. Bonus Prose**
```sql
SELECT d.deal_type, COUNT(*) AS bonus_prose_deals
FROM deals d
WHERE [bonus prose predicate]
GROUP BY d.deal_type
ORDER BY bonus_prose_deals DESC;
```

```text
deal_type            bonus_prose_deals
vs                   44
percentage_of_gross  6
percentage_of_net    1
```

Total bonus-prose deals: `51`.

**3. Dispute Overlap**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, st.status, d.deal_type,
       d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL AS has_bonuses_json,
       d.deal_notes_freetext,
       st.recoups_json
FROM settlements st
JOIN shows s ON s.id = st.show_id
JOIN deals d ON d.show_id = s.id
WHERE (st.status='revised' OR st.recoups_json LIKE '%"status":"disputed"%')
AND [bonus prose predicate]
ORDER BY s.date DESC;
```

```text
show_0308 | 2026-03-17 | paid     | vs                  | has_json=1 | walkout pot prose | disputed production_overage
show_0079 | 2025-05-31 | disputed | percentage_of_gross | has_json=1 | sellout bonus     | disputed production_overage
show_coastal_spell_dispute | 2025-03-14 | disputed | vs | has_json=1 | +$1,000 bonus + marketing recoup | disputed marketing
show_0047 | 2024-06-21 | paid     | vs                  | has_json=0 | Performance bonuses per memo | disputed production_overage
```

Only `4` overlap rows. This class is real, but the evidence is mostly prose-vs-structured mismatch rather than settlement-status overlap.

**4. Positive Regression Candidates**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, ar.name, ag.name, ay.name,
       d.deal_type, d.expense_cap, d.hospitality_cap,
       d.bonuses_json, d.deal_notes_freetext
FROM deals d
JOIN shows s ON s.id=d.show_id
LEFT JOIN artists ar ON ar.id=s.artist_id
LEFT JOIN agents ag ON ag.id=ar.agent_id
LEFT JOIN agencies ay ON ay.id=ag.agency_id
WHERE d.bonuses_json IS NULL
AND d.deal_type='vs'
AND [bonus prose predicate]
ORDER BY s.date DESC;
```

Count: `15`.

Good positives:

```text
show_0064 | 2024-08-09 | Minor Holiday | Rosa Jimenez | Wasserman
vs | expense_cap=2350 | bonuses_json=NULL
"$4,747 guarantee vs 80% of net after expenses... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0167 | 2026-06-09 | Post Hill | Cass Burke | Independent
vs | expense_cap=700 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Expenses capped $700. Hospitality cap $600. Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0014 | 2026-05-29 | Cold Comfort | Kev Park | Paradigm
vs | expense_cap=1850 | bonuses_json=NULL
"3,665 g'tee vs 80% of net. Expenses to 1850. Hospitality $400. Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0094 | 2026-03-31 | Cold Comfort | Kev Park | Paradigm
vs | expense_cap=1650 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0047 | 2024-06-21 | The First Light | Chris Lockhart | CAA
vs | expense_cap=1950 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."
```

**5. Negative Regression Candidates**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, ar.name, ag.name, ay.name,
       d.deal_type, d.expense_cap, d.hospitality_cap,
       d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL AS has_bonuses_json,
       d.deal_notes_freetext
FROM deals d
JOIN shows s ON s.id=d.show_id
LEFT JOIN artists ar ON ar.id=s.artist_id
LEFT JOIN agents ag ON ag.id=ar.agent_id
LEFT JOIN agencies ay ON ay.id=ag.agency_id
WHERE (d.expense_cap IS NOT NULL OR d.hospitality_cap IS NOT NULL)
AND NOT ([bonus prose predicate])
ORDER BY s.date DESC
LIMIT 16;
```

Count: `294`.

Good negatives:

```text
show_0056 | 2026-07-15 | August Haze | Meera Patel | CAA
vs | expense_cap=1600 | hospitality=400 | has_json=0
"Deal: $3,161 vs 85/15 after expenses. Expense cap 1600, hospitality cap 400."

show_0114 | 2026-07-14 | Orenda | Daniel Hwang | WME
percentage_of_net | expense_cap=4450 | hospitality=500 | has_json=0
"90% of net after expenses. Expenses capped $4450. No guarantee."

show_0354 | 2026-06-24 | Post Hill | Cass Burke | Independent
vs | expense_cap=650 | hospitality=500 | has_json=0
"$1,269 guarantee vs 70% of net after expenses... Hospitality cap $500."

show_0347 | 2026-06-03 | North Blue | Maya Okafor | Independent
vs | expense_cap=600 | hospitality=500 | has_json=0
"Deal: $1,182 vs 85/15 after expenses. Expense cap 600, hospitality cap 500."

show_0264 | 2026-05-31 | Stoneflower | Rosa Jimenez | Wasserman
vs | expense_cap=1300 | hospitality=300 | has_json=0
"$2,554 guarantee vs 75% of net after expenses... Hospitality cap $300."
```

Grounded read: `bonus_structure_drift` should probably start as a deterministic gate on `deal_type='vs'`, `bonuses_json IS NULL`, and bonus-related prose. The seed’s strongest positive phrase is vague but intentional: `"Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."`
````

````
No detector work yet. No files changed. I ran read-only `sqlite3 -header -column data/greenroom.db` queries.

**Bonus Prose Predicate**
```sql
lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%bonus%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%walkout%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%threshold%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%gross above%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%over $%'
OR lower(coalesce(d.deal_notes_freetext,'')) LIKE '%pot%'
```

**1. Structured Bonuses**
```sql
SELECT d.deal_type, COUNT(*) AS deals_with_structured_bonuses
FROM deals d
WHERE d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY d.deal_type
ORDER BY deals_with_structured_bonuses DESC;
```

```text
deal_type            deals_with_structured_bonuses
vs                   103
flat                 35
percentage_of_gross  6
percentage_of_net    5
```

Total structured bonus deals: `149`.

**2. Bonus Prose**
```sql
SELECT d.deal_type, COUNT(*) AS bonus_prose_deals
FROM deals d
WHERE [bonus prose predicate]
GROUP BY d.deal_type
ORDER BY bonus_prose_deals DESC;
```

```text
deal_type            bonus_prose_deals
vs                   44
percentage_of_gross  6
percentage_of_net    1
```

Total bonus-prose deals: `51`.

**3. Dispute Overlap**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, st.status, d.deal_type,
       d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL AS has_bonuses_json,
       d.deal_notes_freetext,
       st.recoups_json
FROM settlements st
JOIN shows s ON s.id = st.show_id
JOIN deals d ON d.show_id = s.id
WHERE (st.status='revised' OR st.recoups_json LIKE '%"status":"disputed"%')
AND [bonus prose predicate]
ORDER BY s.date DESC;
```

```text
show_0308 | 2026-03-17 | paid     | vs                  | has_json=1 | walkout pot prose | disputed production_overage
show_0079 | 2025-05-31 | disputed | percentage_of_gross | has_json=1 | sellout bonus     | disputed production_overage
show_coastal_spell_dispute | 2025-03-14 | disputed | vs | has_json=1 | +$1,000 bonus + marketing recoup | disputed marketing
show_0047 | 2024-06-21 | paid     | vs                  | has_json=0 | Performance bonuses per memo | disputed production_overage
```

Only `4` overlap rows. This class is real, but the evidence is mostly prose-vs-structured mismatch rather than settlement-status overlap.

**4. Positive Regression Candidates**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, ar.name, ag.name, ay.name,
       d.deal_type, d.expense_cap, d.hospitality_cap,
       d.bonuses_json, d.deal_notes_freetext
FROM deals d
JOIN shows s ON s.id=d.show_id
LEFT JOIN artists ar ON ar.id=s.artist_id
LEFT JOIN agents ag ON ag.id=ar.agent_id
LEFT JOIN agencies ay ON ay.id=ag.agency_id
WHERE d.bonuses_json IS NULL
AND d.deal_type='vs'
AND [bonus prose predicate]
ORDER BY s.date DESC;
```

Count: `15`.

Good positives:

```text
show_0064 | 2024-08-09 | Minor Holiday | Rosa Jimenez | Wasserman
vs | expense_cap=2350 | bonuses_json=NULL
"$4,747 guarantee vs 80% of net after expenses... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

vs | expense_cap=700 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Expenses capped $700. Hospitality cap $600. Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

vs | expense_cap=1850 | bonuses_json=NULL
"3,665 g'tee vs 80% of net. Expenses to 1850. Hospitality $400. Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0094 | 2026-03-31 | Cold Comfort | Kev Park | Paradigm
vs | expense_cap=1650 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."

show_0047 | 2024-06-21 | The First Light | Chris Lockhart | CAA
vs | expense_cap=1950 | bonuses_json=NULL
"... Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."
```

**5. Negative Regression Candidates**
```sql
SELECT s.id, s.date, ar.name, ag.name, ay.name,
       d.deal_type, d.expense_cap, d.hospitality_cap,
       d.bonuses_json IS NOT NULL AS has_bonuses_json,
       d.deal_notes_freetext
FROM deals d
JOIN shows s ON s.id=d.show_id
LEFT JOIN artists ar ON ar.id=s.artist_id
LEFT JOIN agents ag ON ag.id=ar.agent_id
LEFT JOIN agencies ay ON ay.id=ag.agency_id
WHERE (d.expense_cap IS NOT NULL OR d.hospitality_cap IS NOT NULL)
AND NOT ([bonus prose predicate])
ORDER BY s.date DESC
LIMIT 16;
```

Count: `294`.

Good negatives:

```text

vs | expense_cap=1600 | hospitality=400 | has_json=0
"Deal: $3,161 vs 85/15 after expenses. Expense cap 1600, hospitality cap 400."

vs | expense_cap=650 | hospitality=500 | has_json=0

vs | expense_cap=600 | hospitality=500 | has_json=0

show_0264 | 2026-05-31 | Stoneflower | Rosa Jimenez | Wasserman
vs | expense_cap=1300 | hospitality=300 | has_json=0
"$2,554 guarantee vs 75% of net after expenses... Hospitality cap $300."
```

Grounded read: `bonus_structure_drift` should probably start as a deterministic gate on `deal_type='vs'`, `bonuses_json IS NULL`, and bonus-related prose. The seed’s strongest positive phrase is vague but intentional: `"Performance bonuses per the deal memo (see email thread)."`

<a id="turn-14"></a>

## Turn 14 — Phase 4 — Bonus detector

- Timestamp: `2026-05-21T13:51:00.493Z`
- Turn ID: `019e4acd-c77d-7430-8c3c-b90528ba1fcb`

### User prompt

````
Good. This confirms bonus_structure_drift should stay narrow.

The class is: bonus language exists in deal prose, but bonuses_json is missing.

This matches the working doc: lower-volume than marketing recoup, medium severity, but important because the math engine cannot trigger a bonus that only lives in prose.

Build bonus_structure_drift as the second detector, but keep v1 disciplined:

1. Pre-LLM gate:
   - Only run if bonusesJson is null.
   - Only run if dealNotesFreetext contains bonus-related language case-insensitively.
   - Terms: bonus, bonuses, walkout, threshold, gross above, over $, pot.

2. Post-LLM gate:
   - detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.
   - detectedPhraseFromDeal must contain one of the bonus-related terms.

3. Output:
   - riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift"
   - severity: "medium"
   - detectedPhraseFromDeal
   - citationShowIds: relevant past show IDs only if there is real bonus-related dispute context. Do not force weak citations.
   - suggestedClarification:
     "Quick clarification before [date]: can you confirm the exact bonus terms from the deal memo, including trigger, amount, and whether it stacks with the guarantee or percentage payout?"

4. Lead sentence:
   Prefer the working-doc template when the phrase contains a specific bonus:
   "[BONUS TYPE] at [TRIGGER CONDITION] mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."

   If the prose is vague, use:
   "Bonus terms are referenced in the deal prose, but bonuses_json is empty. Settlement may miss or misapply the bonus unless the deal memo is clarified before show night."

5. Do not use past disputes as the main basis for this detector. The data overlap is thin. This card is about missing structure, not precedent.

6. Add this in the smallest way possible. Follow the marketing_recoup_cap pattern, but avoid building a big abstraction layer yet.

Before applying, show me the proposed diff.

After implementation, run this regression suite:

Positive cases, should return one MEDIUM bonus_structure_drift risk:
- show_0064
- show_0167
- show_0014
- show_0094
- show_0047

Negative cases, should return zero bonus_structure_drift risks:
- show_0056
- show_0114
- show_0354
- show_0347
- show_0264

Do not build production_overage_cap or agent_risk_pattern yet.

@@
 export type MarketingRecoupCapRisk = {
   riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
   severity: "high" | "medium";
   leadSentence: string;
   detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
   suggestedClarification: string;
   citationShowIds: string[];
 };
 
+export type BonusStructureDriftRisk = {
+  riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift";
+  severity: "medium";
+  leadSentence: string;
+  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+  suggestedClarification: string;
+  citationShowIds: string[];
+};
+
+export type PreFlightRisk = MarketingRecoupCapRisk | BonusStructureDriftRisk;
+
@@
 function containsMarketingRecoupSignal(value: string) {
   const normalized = value.toLowerCase();
   return normalized.includes("recoup") || normalized.includes("marketing");
 }
 
+function containsBonusStructureSignal(value: string) {
+  const normalized = value.toLowerCase();
+  return (
+    normalized.includes("bonus") ||
+    normalized.includes("walkout") ||
+    normalized.includes("threshold") ||
+    normalized.includes("gross above") ||
+    normalized.includes("over $") ||
+    normalized.includes("pot")
+  );
+}
+
+function bonusStructureQuestion(showDate: string) {
+  return (
+    `Quick clarification before ${formatShortShowDate(showDate)}: ` +
+    "can you confirm the exact bonus terms from the deal memo, including trigger, " +
+    "amount, and whether it stacks with the guarantee or percentage payout?"
+  );
+}
+
+function bonusLeadSentence(phrase: string) {
+  const normalized = phrase.toLowerCase();
+  const grossAbove = phrase.match(/gross above\s+(\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/i);
+
+  if (normalized.includes("walkout") && grossAbove) {
+    return (
+      `Walkout pot at ${grossAbove[0]} mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. ` +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("threshold")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus threshold mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("pot")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus pot mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  return (
+    "Bonus terms are referenced in the deal prose, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+    "Settlement may miss or misapply the bonus unless the deal memo is clarified before show night."
+  );
+}

+    .filter((r) => {

+
+export async function detectBonusStructureDriftRisks(
+  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
+): Promise<BonusStructureDriftRisk[]> {
+  const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
+  if (!prose || context.target.deal.bonusesJson != null) return [];
+  if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(prose)) return [];
+
+  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
+  if (!apiKey) {
+    throw new Error("OPENAI_API_KEY is required to run pre-flight detection.");
+  }
+
+  const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
+    method: "POST",
+    headers: {
+      "content-type": "application/json",
+      authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify({
+      model: MODEL,
+      temperature: 0.1,
+      response_format: {
+        type: "json_schema",
+        json_schema: {
+          name: "bonus_structure_drift_detection",
+          strict: true,
+          schema: {
+            type: "object",
+            additionalProperties: false,
+            required: ["risks"],
+            properties: {
+              risks: {
+                type: "array",
+                items: {
+                  type: "object",
+                  additionalProperties: false,
+                  required: [
+                    "riskClass",
+                    "detectedPhraseFromDeal",
+                    "citationShowIds",
+                  ],
+                  properties: {
+                    riskClass: {
+                      type: "string",
+                      enum: ["bonus_structure_drift"],
+                    },
+                    detectedPhraseFromDeal: { type: "string" },
+                    citationShowIds: {
+                      type: "array",
+                      items: { type: "string" },
+                    },
+                  },
+                },
+              },
+            },
+          },
+        },
+      },
+      messages: [
+        {
+          role: "system",
+          content: [
+            "You detect only the bonus_structure_drift risk class for Greenroom pre-flight deal review.",
+            "Hard rules:",
+            "1. Never invent values, dates, agency names, amounts, or show IDs.",
+            "2. detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.",
+            "3. citationShowIds must come only from the provided pastBonusDisputes input, and only if directly relevant.",
+            "4. Return strict JSON matching the schema.",
+            "5. No prose explanations outside JSON.",
+          ].join("\n"),
+        },
+        {
+          role: "user",
+          content: JSON.stringify({
+            task: "Return one risk only if bonusesJson is null and target deal prose references bonus terms. Otherwise return an empty risks array.",
+            target: context.target,
+            pastBonusDisputes: context.pastBonusDisputes,
+          }),
+        },
+      ],
+    }),
+  });
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    const text = await response.text();
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI request failed (${response.status}): ${text}`);
+  }
+
+  const completion = await response.json();
+  const message = completion.choices?.[0]?.message;
+  if (message?.refusal) {
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI refusal: ${message.refusal}`);
+  }
+  if (!message?.content) return [];
+
+  const parsed = JSON.parse(message.content) as {
+    risks: {
+      riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift";
+      detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+      citationShowIds: string[];
+    }[];
+  };
+  const citationIds = new Set(context.pastBonusDisputes.map((d) => d.showId));
+
+  return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): BonusStructureDriftRisk[] => {
+    if (risk.riskClass !== "bonus_structure_drift") return [];
+    if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+    if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+
+    return [
+      {
+        riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift",
+        severity: "medium",
+        leadSentence: bonusLeadSentence(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal),
+        detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+        suggestedClarification: bonusStructureQuestion(context.target.date),
+        citationShowIds: risk.citationShowIds.filter((id) =>
+          citationIds.has(id),
+        ),
+      },
+    ];
+  });
+}

 export type MarketingRecoupCapRisk = {
   riskClass: "marketing_recoup_cap";
   severity: "high" | "medium";
   leadSentence: string;
   detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
   suggestedClarification: string;

+export type BonusStructureDriftRisk = {
+  riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift";
+  severity: "medium";
+  leadSentence: string;
+  detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+  suggestedClarification: string;

@@
 function containsMarketingRecoupSignal(value: string) {
   const normalized = value.toLowerCase();
   return normalized.includes("recoup") || normalized.includes("marketing");
 }
 
+function containsBonusStructureSignal(value: string) {
+  const normalized = value.toLowerCase();
+  return (
+    normalized.includes("bonus") ||
+    normalized.includes("bonuses") ||
+    normalized.includes("walkout") ||
+    normalized.includes("threshold") ||
+    normalized.includes("gross above") ||
+    normalized.includes("over $") ||
+    normalized.includes("pot")
+  );
+}
+
+function bonusStructureQuestion(showDate: string) {
+  return (
+    `Quick clarification before ${formatShortShowDate(showDate)}: ` +
+    "can you confirm the exact bonus terms from the deal memo, including trigger, " +
+    "amount, and whether it stacks with the guarantee or percentage payout?"
+  );
+}
+
+function bonusLeadSentence(phrase: string) {
+  const normalized = phrase.toLowerCase();
+  const grossAbove = phrase.match(/gross above\s+(\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/i);
+
+  if (normalized.includes("walkout") && grossAbove) {
+    return (
+      `Walkout pot at ${grossAbove[0]} mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. ` +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("threshold")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus threshold mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("pot")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus pot mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  return (
+    "Bonus terms are referenced in the deal prose, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+    "Settlement may miss or misapply the bonus unless the deal memo is clarified before show night."
+  );
+}
+
 export async function getShowContextForDetection(showId: string) {
@@
 export async function detectMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
   context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
 ): Promise<MarketingRecoupCapRisk[]> {
@@
   });
 }
+
+export async function detectBonusStructureDriftRisks(
+  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
+): Promise<BonusStructureDriftRisk[]> {
+  const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
+  if (!prose || context.target.deal.bonusesJson != null) return [];
+  if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(prose)) return [];
+
+  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
+  if (!apiKey) {
+    throw new Error("OPENAI_API_KEY is required to run pre-flight detection.");
+  }
+
+  const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
+    method: "POST",
+    headers: {
+      "content-type": "application/json",
+      authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify({
+      model: MODEL,
+      temperature: 0.1,
+      response_format: {
+        type: "json_schema",
+        json_schema: {
+          name: "bonus_structure_drift_detection",
+          strict: true,
+          schema: {
+            type: "object",
+            additionalProperties: false,
+            required: ["risks"],
+            properties: {
+              risks: {
+                type: "array",
+                items: {
+                  type: "object",
+                  additionalProperties: false,
+                  required: ["riskClass", "detectedPhraseFromDeal"],
+                  properties: {
+                    riskClass: {
+                      type: "string",
+                      enum: ["bonus_structure_drift"],
+                    },
+                    detectedPhraseFromDeal: { type: "string" },
+                  },
+                },
+              },
+            },
+          },
+        },
+      },
+      messages: [
+        {
+          role: "system",
+          content: [
+            "You detect only the bonus_structure_drift risk class for Greenroom pre-flight deal review.",
+            "Hard rules:",
+            "1. Never invent values, dates, agency names, amounts, or show IDs.",
+            "2. detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.",
+            "3. Return strict JSON matching the schema.",
+            "4. No prose explanations outside JSON.",
+          ].join("\n"),
+        },
+        {
+          role: "user",
+          content: JSON.stringify({
+            task: "Return one risk only if bonusesJson is null and target deal prose references bonus terms. Otherwise return an empty risks array.",
+            target: context.target,
+          }),
+        },
+      ],
+    }),
+  });
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    const text = await response.text();
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI request failed (${response.status}): ${text}`);
+  }
+
+  const completion = await response.json();
+  const message = completion.choices?.[0]?.message;
+  if (message?.refusal) {
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI refusal: ${message.refusal}`);
+  }
+  if (!message?.content) return [];
+
+  const parsed = JSON.parse(message.content) as {
+    risks: {
+      riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift";
+      detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+    }[];
+  };
+
+  return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): BonusStructureDriftRisk[] => {
+    if (risk.riskClass !== "bonus_structure_drift") return [];
+    if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+    if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+
+    return [
+      {
+        riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift",
+        severity: "medium",
+        leadSentence: bonusLeadSentence(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal),
+        detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+        suggestedClarification: bonusStructureQuestion(context.target.date),
+        citationShowIds: [],
+      },
+    ];
+  });
+}
```

+    "amount, and whether it stacks with the guarantee or percentage payout?"
+  );
+}
+
+function bonusLeadSentence(phrase: string) {
+  const normalized = phrase.toLowerCase();
+  const grossAbove = phrase.match(/gross above\s+(\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?)/i);
+
+  if (normalized.includes("walkout") && grossAbove) {
+    return (
+      `Walkout pot at ${grossAbove[0]} mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. ` +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("threshold")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus threshold mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  if (normalized.includes("pot")) {
+    return (
+      "Bonus pot mentioned in deal notes, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+      "The math engine won't trigger this when settlement runs."
+    );
+  }
+
+  return (
+    "Bonus terms are referenced in the deal prose, but bonuses_json is empty. " +
+    "Settlement may miss or misapply the bonus unless the deal memo is clarified before show night."
+  );
+}
+
 export async function getShowContextForDetection(showId: string) {
@@
   });
 }
 
+export async function detectBonusStructureDriftRisks(
+  context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
+): Promise<BonusStructureDriftRisk[]> {
+  const prose = context.target.deal.dealNotesFreetext;
+  if (!prose || context.target.deal.bonusesJson != null) return [];
+  if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(prose)) return [];
+
+  const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
+  if (!apiKey) {
+    throw new Error("OPENAI_API_KEY is required to run pre-flight detection.");
+  }
+
+  const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", {
+    method: "POST",
+    headers: {
+      "content-type": "application/json",
+      authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}`,
+    },
+    body: JSON.stringify({
+      model: MODEL,
+      temperature: 0.1,
+      response_format: {
+        type: "json_schema",
+        json_schema: {
+          name: "bonus_structure_drift_detection",
+          strict: true,
+          schema: {
+            type: "object",
+            additionalProperties: false,
+            required: ["risks"],
+            properties: {
+              risks: {
+                type: "array",
+                items: {
+                  type: "object",
+                  additionalProperties: false,
+                  required: ["riskClass", "detectedPhraseFromDeal"],
+                  properties: {
+                    riskClass: {
+                      type: "string",
+                      enum: ["bonus_structure_drift"],
+                    },
+                    detectedPhraseFromDeal: { type: "string" },
+                  },
+                },
+              },
+            },
+          },
+        },
+      },
+      messages: [
+        {
+          role: "system",
+          content: [
+            "You detect only the bonus_structure_drift risk class for Greenroom pre-flight deal review.",
+            "Hard rules:",
+            "1. Never invent values, dates, agency names, amounts, or show IDs.",
+            "2. detectedPhraseFromDeal must be a literal substring of dealNotesFreetext.",
+            "3. Return strict JSON matching the schema.",
+            "4. No prose explanations outside JSON.",
+          ].join("\n"),
+        },
+        {
+          role: "user",
+          content: JSON.stringify({
+            task: "Return one risk only if bonusesJson is null and target deal prose references bonus terms. Otherwise return an empty risks array.",
+            target: context.target,
+          }),
+        },
+      ],
+    }),
+  });
+
+  if (!response.ok) {
+    const text = await response.text();
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI request failed (${response.status}): ${text}`);
+  }
+
+  const completion = await response.json();
+  const message = completion.choices?.[0]?.message;
+  if (message?.refusal) {
+    throw new Error(`OpenAI refusal: ${message.refusal}`);
+  }
+  if (!message?.content) return [];
+
+  const parsed = JSON.parse(message.content) as {
+    risks: {
+      riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift";
+      detectedPhraseFromDeal: string;
+    }[];
+  };
+
+  return parsed.risks.flatMap((risk): BonusStructureDriftRisk[] => {
+    if (risk.riskClass !== "bonus_structure_drift") return [];
+    if (!containsLiteralSubstring(prose, risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+    if (!containsBonusStructureSignal(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal)) return [];
+
+    return [
+      {
+        riskClass: "bonus_structure_drift",
+        severity: "medium",
+        leadSentence: bonusLeadSentence(risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal),
+        detectedPhraseFromDeal: risk.detectedPhraseFromDeal,
+        suggestedClarification: bonusStructureQuestion(context.target.date),
+        citationShowIds: [],
+      },
+    ];
+  });
+}
+
 export async function persistMarketingRecoupCapRisks(
   context: PreFlightDetectionContext,
   risks: MarketingRecoupCapRisk[],
*** End Patch

*** Add File: scripts/verify-bonus-preflight.ts
+import { readFileSync, existsSync } from "node:fs";
+import {
+  detectBonusStructureDriftRisks,
+  getShowContextForDetection,
+} from "../lib/preFlight";
+import { client } from "../db";
+
+function loadEnvLocal() {
+  const path = ".env.local";
+  if (!existsSync(path)) return;
+
+  const raw = readFileSync(path, "utf8");
+  for (const line of raw.split(/\r?\n/)) {
+    const trimmed = line.trim();
+    if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith("#")) continue;
+    const match = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)=(.*)$/);
+    if (!match) continue;
+
+    const [, key, rawValue] = match;
+    if (process.env[key] != null) continue;
+
+    let value = rawValue.trim();
+    if (
+      (value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
+      (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'"))
+    ) {
+      value = value.slice(1, -1);
+    }
+    process.env[key] = value;
+  }
+}
+
+async function main() {
+  loadEnvLocal();
+
+  const showId = process.argv.slice(2).find((arg) => !arg.startsWith("--"));
+  if (!showId) {
+    throw new Error("Usage: npm run preflight:check:bonus -- <showId>");
+  }
+
+  const context = await getShowContextForDetection(showId);
+  const risks = await detectBonusStructureDriftRisks(context);
+  console.log(JSON.stringify(risks, null, 2));
+}
+
+main()
+  .then(() => {
+    client.close();
+    process.exit(0);
+  })
+  .catch((error) => {
+    console.error(error);
+    client.close();
+    process.exit(1);
+  });
*** End Patch
````

<a id="turn-17"></a>

## Turn 17 — Phase 4 — Bonus detector

- Timestamp: `2026-05-21T14:06:07.461Z`
- Turn ID: `019e4adb-9e62-78d1-9e0c-d9ea4a404358`

### User prompt

````
Bonus detector regression is clean. Do not expand it further.

Now pause before building the third detector.

Summarize the current state of the pre-flight detection layer in 8-12 bullets:
- what marketing_recoup_cap detects
- what bonus_structure_drift detects
- what each detector deliberately does not try to solve
- the regression evidence for each
- the deterministic gates added
- the false-positive bug we caught and fixed
- why bonus drift is medium severity while marketing recoup can be high
- what remains before moving to the UI

No code changes.
````

### Agent messages

#### Agent message 1

````
- `marketing_recoup_cap` detects deals where `dealNotesFreetext` contains actual recoup or marketing language and the structured deal has an `expenseCap`. It is about clarifying whether a marketing recoup sits inside or outside the cap.

- `marketing_recoup_cap` deliberately does not infer risk from expense caps alone, settlement history alone, or agent history alone. The deal prose must contain the ambiguity trigger.

- `bonus_structure_drift` detects deals where bonus-related language exists in `dealNotesFreetext` but `bonusesJson` is null. It is about prose-only bonus terms that the math engine cannot trigger.

- `bonus_structure_drift` deliberately does not use past disputes as its basis, does not cite weak precedent, and does not try to parse the actual bonus structure into settlement math yet.

- Marketing regression evidence is now clean: `show_coastal_spell_dispute` and `show_0141` return HIGH risks; `show_0064`, `show_0001`, and `show_0010` return zero marketing risks.

- Bonus regression evidence is clean: `show_0064`, `show_0167`, `show_0014`, `show_0094`, and `show_0047` each return one MEDIUM `bonus_structure_drift`; `show_0056`, `show_0114`, `show_0354`, `show_0347`, and `show_0264` return zero.

- Marketing now has a pre-LLM gate requiring `dealNotesFreetext` to contain `recoup` or `marketing`, plus a post-LLM gate requiring the detected phrase itself to contain `recoup` or `marketing`.

- Bonus drift has a pre-LLM gate requiring `bonusesJson === null` and bonus-language prose, plus a post-LLM gate requiring a literal substring that contains one of: `bonus`, `bonuses`, `walkout`, `threshold`, `gross above`, `over $`, or `pot`.

- The false-positive bug was that the marketing detector fired on expense-cap language because the LLM was too eager and the literal-substring guard allowed real but wrong phrases like `"Expenses capped $2350"`. The fix was deterministic recoup/marketing gates before and after the model.

- Marketing can be HIGH because the data has concrete precedent with agency, date, and disputed recoup amount. Bonus drift stays MEDIUM because the evidence is missing structure, not a strong dispute-precedent pattern.

- Both detectors keep mechanical copy out of the LLM where possible: lead/severity logic and clarification questions are deterministic templates.

- Remaining before UI: third detector decision/build for `agent_risk_pattern`, persistence path for bonus risks if needed, a combined detection orchestration path, and queries/components to render pending/resolved clarification cards on the show detail page.
````

- `bonus_structure_drift` detects deals where bonus-related language exists in `dealNotesFreetext` but `bonusesJson` is null. It is about prose-only bonus terms that the math engine cannot trigger.

- `bonus_structure_drift` deliberately does not use past disputes as its basis, does not cite weak precedent, and does not try to parse the actual bonus structure into settlement math yet.

- Marketing regression evidence is now clean: `show_coastal_spell_dispute` and `show_0141` return HIGH risks; `show_0064`, `show_0001`, and `show_0010` return zero marketing risks.

- Bonus regression evidence is clean: `show_0064`, `show_0167`, `show_0014`, `show_0094`, and `show_0047` each return one MEDIUM `bonus_structure_drift`; `show_0056`, `show_0114`, `show_0354`, `show_0347`, and `show_0264` return zero.

- Marketing now has a pre-LLM gate requiring `dealNotesFreetext` to contain `recoup` or `marketing`, plus a post-LLM gate requiring the detected phrase itself to contain `recoup` or `marketing`.

- Bonus drift has a pre-LLM gate requiring `bonusesJson === null` and bonus-language prose, plus a post-LLM gate requiring a literal substring that contains one of: `bonus`, `bonuses`, `walkout`, `threshold`, `gross above`, `over $`, or `pot`.

- The false-positive bug was that the marketing detector fired on expense-cap language because the LLM was too eager and the literal-substring guard allowed real but wrong phrases like `"Expenses capped $2350"`. The fix was deterministic recoup/marketing gates before and after the model.

- Marketing can be HIGH because the data has concrete precedent with agency, date, and disputed recoup amount. Bonus drift stays MEDIUM because the evidence is missing structure, not a strong dispute-precedent pattern.
